Learn to detect objects and build real-time AI vision pre-trained models and Roboflow. This hands-on session covers everything from data annotation to model training.

Difficulty Level: Introductory–Plus

Participants will receive a guide to set up their Roboflow and Google Colab environments. Please complete this beforehand to ensure a smooth hands-on experience.

👨‍💻 Ganessa — AI Analyst Intern
👩‍💻 Sara — Data/AI Intern

caption:
🚀 Build a Document-Aware AI: LangChain RAG Workshop
Move beyond basic chatbots. Join this hands-on workshop to build an Agentic RAG pipeline—creating AI that "reads" your data and reasons through complex queries with precision.

📅 Essential Details
Date: 8th April
Location: Agmo Space
Who: Exclusive for MMU Students (Basic Python recommended)
Cost: FREE (Refreshments provided)
Workshop level: Beginner to intermediate 

🛠 What You’ll Master
* LangChain & Gemini 3 Flash: Setting up professional-grade AI tools.
* Vector Databases: Using FAISS to turn text into searchable data.
* Agentic Reasoning: Teaching AI to "decide" when to use its own knowledge vs. your data.
* Structured Output: Ensuring citations and confidence scores.

🛤️ Your AI Engineering Roadmap
We won't just leave you with an introduction. At the end of our session, you'll receive a step-by-step roadmap to transition into AI Engineering. Our workshop notebook also includes a curated list of top-tier courses and materials to keep your momentum going.

⚙️ Requirement & Pre-Setup
Bring: Laptop + Google Account.

🔗 Luma registration link : https://luma.com/jj5cgrkl

Don’t just use AI, build it. See you there!

It might not cover every edge case, but it sets a solid base.

caption:
🚀 Automation with n8n Workshop @ DATAi
Learn how to automate tasks, streamline processes, and build powerful workflows using n8n, one of the leading open-source automation platforms.

This workshop is designed for beginners who want to understand automation through practical, hands-on experience. You’ll get to build real workflows while learning how to integrate tools and automate everyday tasks.

What You’ll Learn:
✨ Core concepts of workflow automation
✨ How to set up and use n8n
✨ Building automated workflows with a visual interface (no heavy coding needed)
✨ Practical examples: productivity, data processing, notifications, and more
✨ Running n8n with Docker and accessing it securely using Cloudflared

Before the Workshop:
•⁠ ⁠Participants will receive a setup guide with instructions to:
•⁠ ⁠Install Docker & Cloudflared
•⁠ ⁠Prepare their environment so n8n is ready to run
(We recommend completing the setup before attending to get the most out of the hands-on session.)

Speakers:
👩‍💻 Hannah — Ex-Intern @ LEAD
👨‍💻 Nareen — Ex-Data Science & AI Engineering Intern @ Juara Partners

Event Details:
📅 8 December 2025
🕗 8:00 PM
📍 AGMO Space, FCM
🍪 Refreshments provided

Join us and start building your first automated workflow with n8n!

Are you interested in programming competitions, event management, web development, multimedia design, marketing related fields? Here's good news for you! We are excited to announce that 𝗖𝗼𝗱𝗲𝗡𝗲𝗰𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗶𝘀 𝗯𝗮𝗰𝗸 𝘁𝗵𝗶𝘀 𝘆𝗲𝗮𝗿, 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐰𝐞 𝐚𝐫𝐞 𝐧𝐨𝐰 𝐜𝐚𝐥𝐥𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐟𝐨𝐫 𝐧𝐞𝐰 𝐜𝐨𝐦𝐦𝐢𝐭𝐭𝐞𝐞 𝐦𝐞𝐦𝐛𝐞𝐫𝐬! 🥳

CodeNection, an annual programming-focused event organised by IT Society MMU Cyberjaya that includes a series of algorithm workshops and competitions. This event will be opened to students of all universities in Malaysia and aims to encourage a competitive mindset within the local programming culture.

❔ 𝐖𝐡𝐨 𝐜𝐚𝐧 𝐣𝐨𝐢𝐧?
We welcome 𝗘𝗩𝗘𝗥𝗬 𝗦𝗧𝗨𝗗𝗘𝗡𝗧𝗦 𝗶𝗻 𝗠𝗠𝗨 𝗖𝘆𝗯𝗲𝗿𝗷𝗮𝘆𝗮 𝗼𝗿 𝗠𝗲𝗹𝗮𝗸𝗮 𝗶𝗻𝘁𝗼 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝘁𝗲𝗮𝗺, 𝗳𝗿𝗼𝗺 𝗮𝗻𝘆 𝗳𝗮𝗰𝘂𝗹𝘁𝗶𝗲𝘀 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗯𝗮𝗰𝗸𝗴𝗿𝗼𝘂𝗻𝗱𝘀, 𝗳𝗿𝗼𝗺 𝗲𝘅𝗽𝗲𝗿𝗶𝗲𝗻𝗰𝗲𝗱 𝘀𝘁𝘂𝗱𝗲𝗻𝘁𝘀 𝘁𝗼 𝘀𝘁𝘂𝗱𝗲𝗻𝘁𝘀 𝘄𝗵𝗼 𝘄𝗮𝗻𝘁 𝘁𝗼 𝗴𝗲𝘁 𝗮 𝗳𝗿𝗲𝘀𝗵 𝗲𝘅𝗽𝗲𝗿𝗶𝗲𝗻𝗰𝗲 𝗼𝗻 𝘄𝗼𝗿𝗸𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗶𝗻 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝘁𝗲𝗮𝗺.

As part of the CodeNection 2023 Team, you will be working closely with the other Committees, and do your best to provide an enjoyable and conducive programming-focused event for students of all universities in Malaysia!

Sign up below and give yourself a shot on what it takes to contribute to the team!

✅ 𝐀𝐩𝐩𝐥𝐢𝐜𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐏𝐞𝐫𝐢𝐨𝐝:

Are you ready to code your way to glory? 🏆

Registration for CodeNection 2023 is now open!! 🥳🎊

If you are a university student in Malaysia and study in Computer Science or Information Technology who loves programming, then you don’t want to miss CodeNection 2023! 💻

CodeNection 2023 is a programming-focused event organised by the IT Society MMU Cyberjaya that includes a series of workshops and programming competitions. You will have the chance to learn new skills, meet new friends, and win amazing prizes! 🎁

Join in our workshops to develop skills, understand how our competitive programming flow will run, and learn about the competition.👨‍💻💡

The event will be open to all university students in Malaysia and will have two categories: Closed (only for MMU students) and Open (for all Malaysian Universities). The competitions will have two rounds: preliminary and final. Only the qualified teams from the preliminary round will be invited to the final round where they will compete for the grand prize. 🏅

Event Highlights: 
👉When: 8th December 2023 - 16th December 2023 
👉Cost: Absolutely FREE !!!
👉What’s included: CodeNection Kits

📆 Registration Period: 6th November 2023 - 30th November 2023

For more information about this awesome event, please visit our website at 🌐: https://www.itscodenection.com/

caption:
👋 Hey everyone, after 7 years of service to the students of the Faculty of Computing and Informatics, it is with great pleasure for us to announce the update for the IT Society logo! In this update, we've kept the distinctive circle encapsulating the letters "i" and "T" to represent the concept of IT Society as first and foremost an organisation that brings together, both FCI students and those interested in Information Technology, in the university. 

We've transformed the lines in the letter "i" to make it more distinctive and recognisable. This transformation emulates the digital lines in MMU's own logo more closely. It also makes the letter "i" look more human – a reminder that we're an organisation made up of students, and that students are at the heart of what we do.

The letter "T" remains largely the same, with a slight change where it meets the letter "i". On the whole, this shapes the letter "T" as a hammer or any other similar tool, representing the importance of Information Technology as a tool in this digital age, and our members' role as its practitioners.

In short, we're the students with the necessary tools for this time and age. It's a reflection of who our members are, a reflection of us as a Society, and a reminder for all of us to keep our skills sharp! 🔥
